note: Email Registration alters the core user forms to log in / register by email and auto-generates usernames. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded): they probe documented facts (the one setting, the generation pattern, the customization hook, the service, the bulk action) an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I want users to log in with their email address instead of a username using the Email Registration module. Do I need any configuration, and can I also allow logging in with either the email OR the username?
evals.expected_output: Login by email works out of the box (the login form's name field is validated by email via user_load_by_mail). To also allow either email or username, set login_with_username = true on the email_registration.settings config object (on the core Account settings form / entity.user.admin_form).

evals.prompt: When someone registers with just an email like jane.doe@example.com, what username does the Email Registration module store, and how are collisions handled?
evals.expected_output: It takes the part of the email before the @ (via email_registration_strip_mail_and_cleanup), cleans illegal characters, giving e.g. 'jane.doe'; email_registration_unique_username() suffixes _1, _2, ... to make it unique.

evals.prompt: In a custom Drupal module I want to change the username that Email Registration auto-generates from the email address. Which hook do I implement (the current, non-deprecated one)?
evals.expected_output: Implement hook_email_registration_name_alter(string &$newAccountName, UserInterface $account). The older hook_email_registration_name() is deprecated (removed in 3.0).

evals.prompt: Which Email Registration service generates a unique placeholder username, and what does the generated value look like?
evals.expected_output: The email_registration.username_generator service (Drupal\email_registration\UsernameGenerator); generateRandomUsername() returns 'email_registration_' followed by a UUID.

evals.prompt: I already have existing users with custom usernames. How can I bulk-regenerate their usernames using Email Registration's own logic from the People admin page?
evals.expected_output: Use the 'Update username (from email_registration)' bulk action (id email_registration_update_username) on the People view (/admin/people); it resets each user to a temporary email_registration_<uuid> name and re-saves, triggering regeneration in hook_user_presave.

evals.prompt: Where do I configure the Email Registration module — what admin route/path holds its settings, and does it add its own permissions?
evals.expected_output: There is no dedicated form; its option lives on the core Account settings form (route entity.user.admin_form, /admin/config/people/accounts). The module defines no permissions of its own.

evals.prompt: With Email Registration enabled, who can still see and edit the username field on the registration/account form?
evals.expected_output: The username field is hidden (rendered as a value element) unless the user has 'change own username' (for their own account) or 'administer users'; otherwise it can be left blank and is auto-generated.
